rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE guarantees by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or remove invalid clauses.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes to avoid scope errors.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type errors, trace the first unresolved identifier on the same line and resolve the root cause.
Ensure AGREE contracts only reference visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex.
Validate that all AGREE scalar type names match AADL syntax (int, real, bool).
Prevent scope mismatches by placing implementation-level guarantees in implementation annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for type-level assumptions and guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings.
AGREE expressions must resolve to valid types; avoid '<error>' type by ensuring all references are valid.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes; move them to implementation annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the model's dependencies.
Do not add architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics; instead, resolve root causes or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about annex placement
AGREE syntax for assume/guarantee contracts in component types
